title: Add optional HTTPRoute sub-reconciler for API exposure
description:
# GitHub Issue #162: Add optional HTTPRoute sub-reconciler for external Keystone API exposure

**Category**: feature | **Scope**: Medium

**Description**: The Keystone API is currently only exposed via a ClusterIP Service on port 5000. External access (CLI users, Horizon, federation partners) requires manual setup outside the operator. Add a new `reconcileHTTPRoute` sub-reconciler that optionally creates a Gateway API `HTTPRoute` (`gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1`) when `spec.gateway` is configured.

The Kubernetes Ingress API (`networking.k8s.io/v1`) is frozen and receives no new features. The ingress-nginx controller has been archived as of March 2026. The Gateway API (GA since October 2023, currently v1.5.x) is the recommended successor and supported by all major implementations (Envoy Gateway, Cilium, Istio, Traefik, NGINX Gateway Fabric).

The operator follows the Gateway API role model: it only creates an **HTTPRoute** (application-developer role). The **GatewayClass** and **Gateway** are infrastructure concerns managed by the platform team.

New `spec.gateway` section:

```yaml
spec:
  gateway:
    parentRef:
      name: openstack-gateway        # required, pre-existing Gateway
      namespace: openstack-infra      # optional, cross-namespace via ReferenceGrant
      sectionName: https              # optional, target a specific Gateway listener
    hostname: keystone.example.com    # required, SNI / Host header match
    path: /                           # optional, default "/"
    annotations: {}                   # optional, pass-through to HTTPRoute metadata
```

Behavior:
- When `spec.gateway` is set, create an HTTPRoute targeting the existing `{name}-api` Service on port 5000
- When `spec.gateway` is removed (nil), delete the HTTPRoute resource
- Derive `status.endpoint` from the hostname: `https://{hostname}/v3` (assume TLS is terminated at the Gateway)
- The existing `spec.publicEndpoint` field takes precedence over the gateway-derived URL for `--bootstrap-public-url` if both are set
- Add an `HTTPRouteReady` condition that reflects whether the route has been accepted by the Gateway controller (check `status.parents[].conditions` for `Accepted=True`)

**Motivation**: Keystone is the central authentication endpoint for all OpenStack services and CLI users. Without operator-managed external exposure, every deployment requires manual creation of routing resources, which drifts from the desired state and is error-prone. The `spec.publicEndpoint` field already exists as a workaround, but the operator cannot ensure that the corresponding network path actually exists. Managing an HTTPRoute declaratively closes this gap, gives the operator a single source of truth for `status.endpoint`, and uses the portable Gateway API standard instead of the frozen Ingress API or controller-specific annotations.

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go` (new `GatewaySpec` struct with `ParentRef`, `Hostname`, `Path`, `Annotations`)
- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go` (validation: hostname required, parentRef.name required)
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_httproute.go` (new file)
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go` (call `reconcileHTTPRoute`, add to owned resources, aggregate condition)
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_networkpolicy.go` (extend ingress rules to allow traffic from the Gateway pod/namespace when both `spec.gateway` and `spec.networkPolicy` are set)
- `operators/keystone/config/crd/bases/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml` (CRD regeneration)
- `operators/keystone/go.mod` (add `sigs.k8s.io/gateway-api` dependency)

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] HTTPRoute is created when `spec.gateway` is set with `parentRef.name` and `hostname`
- [ ] HTTPRoute is deleted when `spec.gateway` is removed
- [ ] HTTPRoute references the existing `{name}-api` Service on port 5000 as `backendRef`
- [ ] `parentRef` supports `name`, `namespace`, and `sectionName`
- [ ] `status.endpoint` reflects `https://{hostname}/v3` when gateway is configured
- [ ] `spec.publicEndpoint` takes precedence over gateway-derived URL for bootstrap
- [ ] `HTTPRouteReady` condition tracks the `Accepted` condition from `status.parents[].conditions`
- [ ] `spec.gateway.annotations` are passed through to the HTTPRoute metadata
- [ ] NetworkPolicy is updated to allow traffic from the Gateway when both `spec.gateway` and `spec.networkPolicy` are set
- [ ] RBAC markers include `gateway.networking.k8s.io/httproutes` permissions
- [ ] Webhook validates that `hostname` and `parentRef.name` are non-empty
- [ ] Unit tests cover creation, deletion, status derivation, and annotation pass-through
- [ ] Integration test verifies end-to-end HTTPRoute lifecycle

**Supersedes**: #161 (Ingress-based approach)

**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/162

stories.title: Platform operator exposes Keystone API via Gateway API HTTPRoute
stories.want: to configure spec.gateway on the Keystone CR so the operator creates an HTTPRoute targeting the existing {name}-api Service on port 5000
stories.so_that: external clients (CLI users, Horizon, federation partners) can reach Keystone without manually creating routing resources
criteria: When spec.gateway is set with parentRef.name and hostname, an HTTPRoute is created in the Keystone CR namespace
HTTPRoute backendRef targets the {name}-api Service on port 5000
HTTPRoute parentRef includes name, and optionally namespace and sectionName from spec.gateway.parentRef
HTTPRoute hostname matches spec.gateway.hostname
HTTPRoute path matches spec.gateway.path (default '/')
HTTPRoute has ownerReference pointing to the Keystone CR for garbage collection

stories.title: Platform operator removes gateway configuration to delete HTTPRoute
stories.want: to remove spec.gateway from the Keystone CR so the operator deletes the HTTPRoute
stories.so_that: the external route is cleaned up declaratively when no longer needed
criteria: When spec.gateway is removed (set to nil), the existing HTTPRoute is deleted
HTTPRouteReady condition is set to True with reason HTTPRouteNotRequired
status.endpoint reverts to cluster-local URL (http://{name}-api.{ns}.svc.cluster.local:5000/v3)
No error occurs if no HTTPRoute existed before removal

stories.title: Platform operator sees gateway-derived endpoint in status
stories.want: status.endpoint to reflect the externally reachable URL when spec.gateway is configured
stories.so_that: I can discover the correct endpoint URL from the CR status without manual calculation
criteria: When spec.gateway is configured, status.endpoint is set to https://{hostname}/v3
When spec.publicEndpoint is also set, it takes precedence over gateway-derived URL for bootstrap --bootstrap-public-url
When spec.gateway is removed, status.endpoint reverts to cluster-local URL
kubectl get keystone shows the correct endpoint in the Endpoint print column

stories.title: Platform operator monitors HTTPRoute acceptance status
stories.want: an HTTPRouteReady condition on the Keystone CR that reflects whether the Gateway controller has accepted the route
stories.so_that: I can detect misconfigurations (wrong Gateway name, missing ReferenceGrant) without inspecting the HTTPRoute directly
criteria: HTTPRouteReady condition is True with reason HTTPRouteAccepted when status.parents[].conditions has Accepted=True
HTTPRouteReady condition is False with reason HTTPRouteNotAccepted when the route is not accepted
HTTPRouteReady condition is True with reason HTTPRouteNotRequired when spec.gateway is nil
HTTPRouteReady participates in the aggregate Ready condition
ObservedGeneration is set on the condition to track spec changes

stories.title: Platform operator passes custom annotations to HTTPRoute
stories.want: to set spec.gateway.annotations so they are passed through to the HTTPRoute metadata
stories.so_that: I can attach implementation-specific configuration (e.g., rate limits, timeouts) without modifying the operator
criteria: Annotations from spec.gateway.annotations are set on the HTTPRoute metadata
Operator-managed labels are not affected by custom annotations
Annotations are updated when spec.gateway.annotations changes
Removing annotations from the spec removes them from the HTTPRoute

stories.title: Platform operator with NetworkPolicy allows Gateway traffic
stories.want: the NetworkPolicy to automatically allow ingress from the Gateway when both spec.gateway and spec.networkPolicy are configured
stories.so_that: external traffic routed through the Gateway can reach Keystone pods without manual NetworkPolicy modifications
criteria: When both spec.gateway and spec.networkPolicy are set, an additional ingress peer is added for the Gateway namespace
The Gateway ingress peer uses spec.gateway.parentRef.namespace (or CR namespace if not set) as namespace selector
When spec.gateway is removed, the Gateway ingress peer is removed from the NetworkPolicy
Existing user-specified ingress sources are preserved

stories.title: Webhook rejects invalid gateway configuration
stories.want: clear validation errors when spec.gateway is misconfigured
stories.so_that: I get immediate feedback on missing required fields instead of silent failures
criteria: Webhook rejects spec.gateway with empty hostname
Webhook rejects spec.gateway with empty parentRef.name
Webhook accepts spec.gateway with only hostname and parentRef.name (all other fields optional)
Validation errors include field paths and descriptive messages

requirements.description: The operator SHALL create an HTTPRoute when spec.gateway is configured with parentRef.name and hostname
requirements.rationale: Core feature requirement — the operator must manage the HTTPRoute lifecycle declaratively

scenarios.name: HTTPRoute created with valid gateway config
scenarios.when: spec.gateway is set with parentRef.name='openstack-gateway' and hostname='keystone.example.com'
scenarios.then: an HTTPRoute is created in the CR namespace with parentRef, hostname match, and backendRef to {name}-api:5000
and_then: HTTPRoute has ownerReference to Keystone CR

scenarios.name: HTTPRoute updated when gateway config changes
scenarios.then: the existing HTTPRoute is updated with the new hostname

scenarios.name: HTTPRoute includes optional parentRef fields
scenarios.then: the HTTPRoute parentRef includes namespace and sectionName fields

requirements.description: The operator SHALL delete the HTTPRoute when spec.gateway is removed (nil)
requirements.rationale: Lifecycle management requires clean deletion when the feature is disabled

scenarios.name: HTTPRoute deleted when gateway removed
scenarios.when: spec.gateway is removed (set to nil) and an HTTPRoute exists
scenarios.then: the HTTPRoute is deleted
and_then: HTTPRouteReady condition is set to True with reason HTTPRouteNotRequired

scenarios.name: No error when no HTTPRoute exists on removal
scenarios.when: spec.gateway is removed and no HTTPRoute exists
scenarios.then: no error occurs and condition is set to HTTPRouteNotRequired

requirements.description: The HTTPRoute SHALL reference the existing {name}-api Service on port 5000 as backendRef
requirements.rationale: The Service is created by reconcileDeployment and is the standard entry point for Keystone API traffic

scenarios.name: BackendRef targets correct service
scenarios.when: an HTTPRoute is created for Keystone CR named 'my-keystone'
scenarios.then: backendRef.name is 'my-keystone-api' and backendRef.port is 5000
and_then: backendRef.group is empty string (core API group)

scenarios.name: Path prefix matches spec.gateway.path
scenarios.then: HTTPRoute rule has PathMatch of type PathPrefix with value '/identity'

scenarios.name: Default path is /
scenarios.then: HTTPRoute rule has PathMatch of type PathPrefix with value '/'

requirements.description: The operator SHALL derive status.endpoint from the gateway hostname as https://{hostname}/v3
requirements.rationale: status.endpoint should reflect the externally reachable URL when gateway routing is configured

scenarios.name: Endpoint derived from gateway hostname
scenarios.when: spec.gateway.hostname is 'keystone.example.com' and deployment is ready
scenarios.then: status.endpoint is 'https://keystone.example.com/v3'

scenarios.name: Endpoint reverts when gateway removed
scenarios.then: status.endpoint reverts to 'http://{name}-api.{ns}.svc.cluster.local:5000/v3'

scenarios.name: publicEndpoint takes precedence for bootstrap
scenarios.when: both spec.gateway.hostname and spec.bootstrap.publicEndpoint are set
scenarios.then: bootstrap --bootstrap-public-url uses spec.bootstrap.publicEndpoint
and_then: status.endpoint still reflects the gateway-derived URL

requirements.description: The operator SHALL track the HTTPRoute Accepted condition from status.parents[].conditions as HTTPRouteReady
requirements.rationale: Operators need visibility into whether the Gateway controller has accepted the route without inspecting the HTTPRoute directly

scenarios.name: HTTPRouteReady reflects Accepted=True
scenarios.then: HTTPRouteReady is set to True with reason HTTPRouteAccepted

scenarios.name: HTTPRouteReady reflects not-accepted state
scenarios.when: the HTTPRoute exists but status.parents[].conditions does not contain Accepted=True
scenarios.then: HTTPRouteReady is set to False with reason HTTPRouteNotAccepted
and_then: reconciler requeues to poll for acceptance

scenarios.name: HTTPRouteReady when gateway not configured
scenarios.then: HTTPRouteReady is set to True with reason HTTPRouteNotRequired

requirements.description: The operator SHALL pass spec.gateway.annotations through to the HTTPRoute metadata
requirements.rationale: Implementation-specific configuration (rate limits, timeouts, CORS) requires annotation pass-through

scenarios.name: Annotations passed through on creation
scenarios.then: the HTTPRoute metadata.annotations includes the annotation

scenarios.name: Annotations updated on change
scenarios.then: the HTTPRoute metadata.annotations is updated to match

scenarios.name: No annotations when field is nil
scenarios.then: the HTTPRoute metadata has no user-specified annotations

requirements.description: The webhook SHALL validate that hostname and parentRef.name are non-empty when spec.gateway is set
requirements.rationale: Missing required fields would produce an invalid HTTPRoute, so validation must catch this early

scenarios.name: Valid gateway config accepted
scenarios.then: validation passes with no errors

scenarios.name: Empty hostname rejected
scenarios.then: validation returns error on field spec.gateway.hostname
and_then: error message indicates hostname is required

scenarios.name: Empty parentRef.name rejected
scenarios.then: validation returns error on field spec.gateway.parentRef.name
and_then: error message indicates parentRef.name is required

scenarios.name: Nil gateway accepted
scenarios.then: validation passes with no errors

requirements.description: The NetworkPolicy SHALL include an ingress peer for Gateway traffic when both spec.gateway and spec.networkPolicy are set
requirements.rationale: Without a NetworkPolicy exception, the Gateway controller cannot route traffic to Keystone pods when NetworkPolicy is enabled

scenarios.name: Gateway ingress peer added when both features enabled
scenarios.when: spec.gateway is set with parentRef.namespace='openstack-infra' and spec.networkPolicy is set
scenarios.then: the NetworkPolicy includes an additional ingress peer with namespaceSelector matching the gateway namespace
and_then: existing user-specified ingress sources are preserved

scenarios.name: Gateway peer uses CR namespace when parentRef.namespace is empty
scenarios.when: spec.gateway is set without parentRef.namespace and spec.networkPolicy is set
scenarios.then: the NetworkPolicy includes an ingress peer with namespaceSelector matching the CR namespace

scenarios.name: No gateway peer when only networkPolicy is set
scenarios.when: spec.networkPolicy is set but spec.gateway is nil
scenarios.then: no gateway-related ingress peer is added to the NetworkPolicy

requirements.description: The operator SHALL include RBAC permissions for gateway.networking.k8s.io/httproutes
requirements.rationale: Without RBAC markers, the controller cannot create, read, or update HTTPRoute resources

scenarios.name: RBAC markers present
scenarios.when: controller code is generated with controller-gen
scenarios.then: RBAC role includes get, list, watch, create, update, patch, delete for gateway.networking.k8s.io/httproutes

scenarios.name: HTTPRoute status readable
scenarios.then: RBAC permissions allow reading HTTPRoute status subresource

requirements.description: The operator SHALL register HTTPRoute as an owned resource and include HTTPRouteReady in condition aggregation
requirements.rationale: Owned resources trigger reconciliation on changes; condition aggregation ensures Ready reflects all sub-conditions

scenarios.name: HTTPRoute changes trigger reconciliation
scenarios.when: an owned HTTPRoute is modified externally
scenarios.then: the Keystone reconciler is triggered to reconcile the CR

scenarios.name: HTTPRouteReady participates in Ready aggregation
scenarios.then: the aggregate Ready condition is False

scenarios.name: HTTPRouteReady=NotRequired does not block Ready
scenarios.when: spec.gateway is nil and HTTPRouteReady is True with reason HTTPRouteNotRequired
scenarios.then: the aggregate Ready condition can be True (if all other sub-conditions are True)

tasks.title: Add gateway-api dependency and GatewaySpec types to keystone_types.go (CC-0065, REQ-001, REQ-007)
tasks.description:
1. Run `go get sigs.k8s.io/gateway-api@latest` in operators/keystone/ to promote gateway-api from indirect to explicit dependency.
2. Add GatewaySpec struct with ParentRefSpec, Hostname, Path, Annotations to keystone_types.go following the NetworkPolicySpec pattern (optional pointer field).
3. Add `Gateway *GatewaySpec` field to KeystoneSpec with +optional tag and doc comment referencing CC-0065.
4. Run `go generate ./...` to regenerate zz_generated.deepcopy.go.
5. Verify `go vet ./...` passes.

New types:
- GatewaySpec: Gateway *GatewaySpec on KeystoneSpec
- ParentRefSpec: Name (required), Namespace (optional), SectionName (optional)
- Path defaults to '/' (doc only, applied in reconciler)

tasks.title: Add webhook validation for spec.gateway fields (CC-0065, REQ-007)
tasks.description:
Add validation to keystone_webhook.go validate() function:
1. When spec.Gateway != nil: validate hostname is non-empty, parentRef.name is non-empty.
2. Use field.Required for empty hostname at specPath.Child('gateway', 'hostname').
3. Use field.Required for empty parentRef.name at specPath.Child('gateway', 'parentRef', 'name').
4. Follow the existing pattern from TrustFlush/NetworkPolicy validation.
5. Add unit tests in keystone_webhook_test.go for: valid gateway, empty hostname, empty parentRef.name, nil gateway.

tasks.title: Implement reconcileHTTPRoute sub-reconciler with create/update/delete lifecycle (CC-0065, REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-005, REQ-006)
tasks.description:
Create new file operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_httproute.go:
1. Implement reconcileHTTPRoute(ctx, keystone) following the reconcileNetworkPolicy pattern.
2. Path 1 (gateway nil): delete existing HTTPRoute via deleteHTTPRoute(), set HTTPRouteReady=True/HTTPRouteNotRequired.
3. Path 2 (gateway set): build HTTPRoute via buildKeystoneHTTPRoute(), ensure via ensureHTTPRoute().
4. buildKeystoneHTTPRoute: construct gatewayv1.HTTPRoute with parentRef (name, optional namespace/sectionName), hostname, path prefix match (default '/'), backendRef to {name}-api:5000, labels from commonLabels(), annotations from spec.gateway.annotations.
5. ensureHTTPRoute: follow ensureNetworkPolicy pattern — Get/Create/Update with owner reference, additive label/annotation merge, spec overwrite, DeepEqual skip.
6. deleteHTTPRoute: follow deleteNetworkPolicy pattern.
7. After ensure, check HTTPRoute status.parents[].conditions for Accepted=True. Set HTTPRouteReady=True/HTTPRouteAccepted or False/HTTPRouteNotAccepted with requeue.
8. Add condition constants (conditionTypeHTTPRouteReady, etc.).

tasks.title: Add buildKeystoneHTTPRoute and lifecycle unit tests (CC-0065, REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-005, REQ-006)
tasks.description:
Create operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_httproute_test.go following the reconcile_networkpolicy_test.go pattern:
1. Test helpers: hrTestScheme() (register gateway-api types), hrTestKeystone(), newHRTestReconciler().
2. Build tests: TestBuildKeystoneHTTPRoute_NameAndNamespace, _Labels, _ParentRef, _ParentRefWithOptionalFields, _Hostname, _DefaultPath, _CustomPath, _BackendRef, _Annotations, _NoAnnotations.
3. Lifecycle tests: TestReconcileHTTPRoute_GatewaySet_CreatesHTTPRoute, _GatewayNil_NoExisting_SetsNotRequired, _GatewayNil_ExistingRoute_DeletesHTTPRoute, _GatewaySet_ConditionObservedGeneration, _GatewaySet_HTTPRouteUpdated, _NoChange_SkipsUpdate.
4. Error tests: _EnsureError_Propagated, _DeleteError_Propagated.
5. Status tests: _AcceptedCondition_True, _AcceptedCondition_False_Requeues.

tasks.title: Wire reconcileHTTPRoute into main controller, RBAC, and condition aggregation (CC-0065, REQ-009, REQ-010)
tasks.description:
Modify operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go:
1. Add import for gatewayv1 'sigs.k8s.io/gateway-api/apis/v1'.
2. Add RBAC marker: +kubebuilder:rbac:groups=gateway.networking.k8s.io,resources=httproutes,verbs=get;list;watch;create;update;patch;delete
3. Add RBAC marker for httproutes/status: +kubebuilder:rbac:groups=gateway.networking.k8s.io,resources=httproutes/status,verbs=get
4. Add 'HTTPRouteReady' to subConditionTypes slice.
5. Add reconcileHTTPRoute call in Reconcile() after reconcileDeployment and before reconcileBootstrap (needs the Service to exist, and should set endpoint before bootstrap uses it).
6. Add Owns(&gatewayv1.HTTPRoute{}) in SetupWithManager().
7. Register gatewayv1 scheme in main.go or suite setup.

tasks.title: Update status.endpoint derivation for gateway hostname (CC-0065, REQ-004)
tasks.description:
Modify operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go:
1. After the deployment-ready status.Endpoint assignment (line 118), add logic: if keystone.Spec.Gateway != nil, override status.Endpoint to fmt.Sprintf('https://%s/v3', keystone.Spec.Gateway.Hostname).
2. The cluster-local URL is the default; gateway-derived URL overrides it.
3. Verify that reconcile_bootstrap.go still uses spec.Bootstrap.PublicEndpoint (it does — no change needed there since bootstrap reads PublicEndpoint directly, not status.Endpoint).
4. Add unit tests in reconcile_deployment_test.go: TestReconcileDeployment_EndpointWithGateway, TestReconcileDeployment_EndpointWithoutGateway.

tasks.title: Extend NetworkPolicy to allow Gateway traffic (CC-0065, REQ-008)
tasks.description:
Modify operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_networkpolicy.go:
1. In buildKeystoneNetworkPolicy(), after building user-specified ingress peers, check if keystone.Spec.Gateway != nil.
2. If gateway is set, add an additional ingress peer: namespaceSelector matching the gateway namespace (spec.gateway.parentRef.namespace if set, otherwise keystone.Namespace) with label kubernetes.io/metadata.name.
3. Add unit tests in reconcile_networkpolicy_test.go: TestBuildKeystoneNetworkPolicy_GatewayIngressPeer, TestBuildKeystoneNetworkPolicy_GatewayIngressPeer_CrossNamespace, TestBuildKeystoneNetworkPolicy_NoGateway_NoExtraPeer.

tasks.title: Add gateway-api scheme registration in main.go and integration test setup (CC-0065, REQ-010)
tasks.description:
1. In operators/keystone/main.go, add gatewayv1 scheme registration: gatewayv1.Install(scheme) or gatewayv1.AddToScheme(scheme).
2. In operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller_test.go testScheme(), add gatewayv1 scheme.
3. In integration_test.go setupEnvTestWithController, ensure gateway-api CRDs are available to envtest (add to scheme and CRD paths).
4. Add Owns(&gatewayv1.HTTPRoute{}) to the integration test controller builder.
5. Verify existing unit tests still pass with the new scheme additions.

tasks.title: Add integration tests for HTTPRoute lifecycle (CC-0065, REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-005)
tasks.description:
Add integration tests in operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go:
1. TestIntegration_HTTPRoute_CreatedWhenGatewaySet: Create Keystone CR with spec.gateway, verify HTTPRoute exists with correct parentRef/hostname/backendRef, verify HTTPRouteReady condition.
2. TestIntegration_HTTPRoute_DeletedWhenGatewayRemoved: Create CR with gateway, then patch to remove gateway, verify HTTPRoute deleted, verify condition set to HTTPRouteNotRequired.
3. TestIntegration_HTTPRoute_UpdatedWhenGatewayChanged: Create CR with gateway, patch hostname, verify HTTPRoute updated.
4. TestIntegration_HTTPRoute_EndpointDerivedFromGateway: Verify status.endpoint shows https://{hostname}/v3.
5. Follow existing integration test patterns (Eventually/pollInterval timeouts, env test client).

tasks.title: Reference documentation for spec.gateway and HTTPRoute sub-reconciler (CC-0065)
tasks.description:
Create docs/reference/keystone-gateway.md:
1. Document GatewaySpec fields (parentRef, hostname, path, annotations) with types, defaults, and constraints.
2. Document HTTPRouteReady condition (reasons: HTTPRouteAccepted, HTTPRouteNotAccepted, HTTPRouteNotRequired).
3. Document status.endpoint derivation (gateway-derived vs cluster-local, precedence with publicEndpoint).
4. Document NetworkPolicy interaction (automatic Gateway ingress peer).
5. Include example CR YAML snippet showing spec.gateway configuration.

test_specifications.story: Platform operator removes gateway configuration to delete HTTPRoute
test_specifications.expected: HTTPRoute deleted when spec.gateway is removed via patch

test_specifications.story: Platform operator sees gateway-derived endpoint in status
test_specifications.expected: status.endpoint shows https://{hostname}/v3 when gateway is configured

review_criteria: HTTPRoute is created with correct parentRef, hostname, path, backendRef when spec.gateway is set
HTTPRoute is deleted when spec.gateway is removed; no orphaned resources
HTTPRouteReady condition participates in Ready aggregation via subConditionTypes
status.endpoint reflects https://{hostname}/v3 when gateway configured, cluster-local URL otherwise
Webhook rejects empty hostname and empty parentRef.name with field-specific errors
NetworkPolicy includes gateway namespace ingress peer when both spec.gateway and spec.networkPolicy are set
RBAC markers include gateway.networking.k8s.io/httproutes with full CRUD verbs
Unit tests cover all three lifecycle paths (create, delete, not-required) plus build function, error propagation, and idempotent update skip
Integration tests verify end-to-end HTTPRoute lifecycle with envtest
go vet and golangci-lint pass without errors on all modified and new files
implementation_notes:
## Architecture

```
                     Keystone CR
                    ┌─────────────────┐
                    │ spec.gateway    │
                    │ spec.networkPol.│
                    └────────┬────────┘
                             │
              Reconcile() ─ sequential sub-reconcilers
                             │
    ┌────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┐
    │                        │                        │
    ▼                        ▼                        ▼
reconcileNetworkPolicy  reconcileDeployment   reconcileHTTPRoute
    │                        │                        │
    ▼                        ▼                        ▼
 NetworkPolicy           Service              HTTPRoute
 (adds gateway           {name}-api:5000       ├─ parentRef → Gateway
  ingress peer)          + Deployment          ├─ hostname match
                         + PDB                 ├─ backendRef → {name}-api:5000
                                               └─ annotations pass-through
                                                      │
                                             status.parents[].conditions
                                                      │
                                                      ▼
                                             HTTPRouteReady condition
```

## Data Flow
1. User sets spec.gateway on Keystone CR
2. Webhook validates hostname and parentRef.name are non-empty
3. reconcileNetworkPolicy adds gateway namespace as ingress peer (if networkPolicy also set)
4. reconcileDeployment creates Service (always), sets status.endpoint to gateway-derived URL
5. reconcileHTTPRoute creates HTTPRoute with parentRef, hostname, path, backendRef, annotations
6. reconcileHTTPRoute reads back HTTPRoute status.parents[].conditions for Accepted
7. Sets HTTPRouteReady condition (True/Accepted or False/NotAccepted with requeue)
8. setReadyCondition aggregates all sub-conditions including HTTPRouteReady

## Key Decisions
- HTTPRoute reconciler runs AFTER reconcileDeployment (Service must exist) and BEFORE reconcileBootstrap (endpoint should be set before bootstrap uses it for public URL fallback)
- status.endpoint uses gateway-derived URL (https://{hostname}/v3) when spec.gateway is set; reverts to cluster-local when removed
- spec.publicEndpoint still takes precedence for bootstrap --bootstrap-public-url (no change to existing precedence logic)
- Gateway namespace ingress peer in NetworkPolicy uses kubernetes.io/metadata.name label selector (standard Kubernetes label) to match the gateway namespace
- HTTPRoute name follows existing convention: {name}-api (same as Service, Deployment, HPA, NetworkPolicy)
- ensureHTTPRoute follows ensureNetworkPolicy pattern: additive metadata merge, authoritative spec overwrite, DeepEqual skip guard

## Pitfalls
- Gateway API CRDs must be pre-installed on the cluster — the operator only creates HTTPRoute, not GatewayClass/Gateway
- Adding HTTPRouteReady to subConditionTypes affects ALL existing Keystone CRs — first reconcile after upgrade must set the condition
- Cross-namespace parentRef requires a ReferenceGrant (out of scope for this operator)
- HTTPRoute acceptance status is populated asynchronously by the Gateway controller — reconciler must handle empty status gracefully
